name: Immune clearance of senescent cells bootstrap for June 2, 2026
summary: Submitted bootstrap bundle establishing the first public baseline candidate for the immune clearance of senescent cells track.

scope.question: What is the current human-relevant evidence landscape for Immune Clearance of Senescent Cells, and what belongs in the first public baseline?

proposed_changes.summary: Add foundational uPAR CAR T senolytic source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source anchors the first engineered immune-clearance proof of concept for senescent cells in vivo.

proposed_changes.summary: Add uPAR CAR T metabolic aging mouse source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source supplies direct physiological-aging mouse evidence for uPAR CAR T effects on fitness and metabolic endpoints.

proposed_changes.summary: Add 2026 uPAR CAR T intestinal aging source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source is the newest direct anti-uPAR CAR T aging-relevant animal signal and expands the evidence into tissue regeneration.

proposed_changes.summary: Add NKG2D-CAR T aged mouse and nonhuman-primate source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source adds an independent CAR T target and the strongest nonhuman-primate bridge in the bundle.

proposed_changes.summary: Add perforin-deficient immune surveillance source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source supports the core premise that cytotoxic immune surveillance restrains senescent-cell accumulation during aging.

proposed_changes.summary: Add HLA-E/NKG2A senescent-cell immune evasion source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source supplies a human tissue and in-vitro immune-checkpoint mechanism without creating an efficacy upgrade.

proposed_changes.summary: Add GD3 senescence immune-checkpoint source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source adds a newer NK-evasion target with anti-GD3 mouse intervention evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add CMV-antigen CD4 T-cell senescent-cell clearance source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source provides human skin and ex vivo evidence for natural immune recognition of senescent cells.

proposed_changes.summary: Normalize anti-uPAR CAR T cells as a preclinical immune-clearance intervention.
proposed_changes.rationale: uPAR CAR T is the largest direct intervention cluster in this track baseline.

proposed_changes.summary: Normalize NKG2D-CAR T cells as a preclinical immune-clearance intervention.
proposed_changes.rationale: NKG2D-CAR T supplies an independent engineered-cell target and a nonhuman-primate bridge.

proposed_changes.summary: Normalize anti-GD3 senescence immunotherapy as a preclinical checkpoint-style intervention.
proposed_changes.rationale: GD3 provides a newer immune-evasion target with mouse intervention evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Normalize HLA-E/NKG2A senescence checkpoint blockade as a human-mechanistic intervention concept.
proposed_changes.rationale: This record keeps human checkpoint biology visible while preserving the in-vitro and safety boundary.

proposed_changes.summary: Add uPAR CAR T pathology-model study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study maps the foundational uPAR CAR T source to the immune-clearance track.

proposed_changes.summary: Add anti-uPAR CAR T metabolic aging study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study anchors physiological-aging mouse function and metabolic endpoints.

proposed_changes.summary: Add anti-uPAR CAR T aged intestinal regeneration study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study captures the newest 2026 tissue-regeneration extension.

proposed_changes.summary: Add NKG2D-CAR T aged mouse and primate study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study adds nonhuman-primate senescent-cell deletion to the baseline while keeping it preclinical.

proposed_changes.summary: Add perforin-deficient immune surveillance study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study provides the mechanistic aging rationale for immune surveillance of senescent cells.

proposed_changes.summary: Add HLA-E/NKG2A senescent-cell evasion study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study records the human tissue and in-vitro checkpoint evidence without treating it as human efficacy.

proposed_changes.summary: Add GD3 senescence immune-checkpoint study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study records the GD3 immune-evasion and anti-GD3 mouse intervention signal.

proposed_changes.summary: Add cytotoxic CD4 CMV senescent human skin study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study captures natural human immune-clearance mechanism evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add foundational uPAR CAR T senolytic finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports the animal_signal stage assignment while preserving disease-model boundaries.

proposed_changes.summary: Add anti-uPAR CAR T aged-mouse metabolic and fitness finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding anchors a direct physiological-aging mouse functional signal.

proposed_changes.summary: Add anti-uPAR CAR T aged intestinal regeneration finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding anchors the newest 2026 aging-relevant CAR T extension.

proposed_changes.summary: Add NKG2D-CAR T aged mouse and primate finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supplies independent engineered immune-clearance evidence and the nonhuman-primate bridge.

proposed_changes.summary: Add perforin immune-surveillance aging mechanism finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports the biological premise that cytotoxic immune surveillance restrains senescent-cell accumulation.

proposed_changes.summary: Add HLA-E/NKG2A senescent-cell evasion finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports human mechanism while limiting the stage to preclinical intervention evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add anti-GD3 senescence immune-checkpoint finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding broadens the intervention set beyond CAR T while remaining animal-stage.

proposed_changes.summary: Add cytotoxic CD4 T-cell CMV senescent-cell clearance finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding gives a human-mechanistic natural clearance signal without treating it as an intervention.

proposed_changes.summary: Add first public immune-clearance track outlook.
proposed_changes.rationale: The staged evidence supports a low-confidence animal_signal baseline with accelerating momentum and explicit human-efficacy and safety boundaries.
uncertainty_flags: Engineered cell therapy evidence remains preclinical
Target specificity and beneficial senescence boundaries remain unresolved
Human mechanism evidence should not be overread as efficacy

proposed_outlook_implications.note: A first public baseline is justified at low-confidence animal_signal because multiple immune-mediated senescent-cell clearance strategies show animal signals, while human evidence remains mechanistic or ex vivo.

Publishing this bundle would broaden cellular senescence coverage beyond small-molecule senolytics and senomorphics by adding immune-mediated clearance, with clear boundaries from human efficacy claims.

next_actions: Publication complete. Review downstream pages if this change affects shared surfaces.
